proposals.title: Quality-Diversity Archive
proposals.rationale: Preserve behaviorally distinct high-performing organisms instead of letting scalar fitness collapse the population into one lineage, sustaining multiple paths to frontier gains.
acceptance: Each organism is deterministically assigned to a documented behavioral niche from genome-derived descriptors.
Each niche retains its strongest artifact while preserving displaced artifacts as immutable history.
Selection includes underexplored niches without allowing candidates below the configured quality floor.
Repeated runs over identical inputs produce byte-identical archive state.

proposals.title: Evolution Attempt Ledger
proposals.rationale: Record rejected as well as accepted mutations so failed experiments become durable provenance, repeated dead ends can be detected, and selection decisions remain auditable.
acceptance: Every evaluated candidate appends a content-addressed record containing parent, challenge, candidate, fitness epoch, score, validation result, and outcome.
Accepted evolution receipts reference the exact attempt digest that produced them.
Submitting an identical attempt is idempotent and does not create another ledger entry.
Ledger verification fails after mutation, deletion, or reordering of any attempt record.

proposals.title: Deterministic Lineage Crossover
proposals.rationale: Allow complementary traits from distinct successful lineages to recombine, creating frontier opportunities unavailable to single-parent mutation while retaining complete multi-parent ancestry.
acceptance: A crossover challenge names two distinct content-addressed parents and a deterministic seed.
The same parents, seed, and operator version always produce the same candidate digest.
Validation prevents incompatible or closely related parent combinations according to documented rules.
Accepted offspring records both parent digests and appears under both ancestry branches without duplicating the artifact.

proposals.title: Stagnation Detection and Recovery
proposals.rationale: Detect plateaus from provenance-backed gain history and adapt mutation pressure without silently weakening acceptance standards, keeping long-running evolution productive.
acceptance: Stagnation is declared deterministically from a configurable number of no-gain attempts or generations.
Recovery escalates documented mutation parameters while preserving the active fitness floor and leap margin.
A successful frontier gain resets recovery state and records the transition in the evolution ledger.
Replay of the same history yields identical stagnation and recovery decisions.

proposals.title: Portable Lineage Proof Bundles
proposals.rationale: Export minimal self-contained ancestry evidence so any artifact's origin, score epoch, and chain of accepted transformations can be verified without trusting or downloading the full warehouse.
acceptance: A command exports a proof bundle for any known artifact digest containing only the required ancestry records and integrity metadata.
Proof verification succeeds offline from an empty working directory using repository code alone.
Verification rejects substituted ancestors, missing links, altered scores, and mismatched fitness versions.
Two exports for the same artifact and ledger revision are byte-identical.

proposals.title: Enforce a unified bounded-autonomy policy
proposals.rationale: Brainstem, Copilot CLI, promotion, and council-driven actions need one fail-closed policy for budgets, allowed operations, and external side effects.
acceptance: A versioned policy limits rounds, runtime, provider calls, and permitted side effects.
Policy violations produce a structured failure without modifying warehouse data or GitHub resources.
Every run receipt records the policy digest and consumed budget.
Identical inputs and policy produce identical authorization decisions.

proposals.title: Gate council recommendations before execution
proposals.rationale: The exact-eight council should inform autonomous work without allowing a recommendation to become an unbounded action or execute against a changed repository.
acceptance: A selected proposal is converted into an immutable execution manifest containing snapshot head, scope, files, acceptance criteria, and budget.
Execution is refused when the repository head differs from the manifest snapshot.
No proposal executes unless it satisfies configured consensus and autonomy thresholds.
Gate decisions are deterministic and stored with the source council digest.

proposals.title: Add resumable idempotent orchestration runs
proposals.rationale: Multi-step local-provider runs must survive interruption without repeating accepted jumps, provider calls, promotions, or other side effects.
acceptance: Each run has a stable ID and persisted stage checkpoints.
Restarting an interrupted run resumes from the last completed stage.
Replaying a completed run performs no duplicate provider call, frontier mutation, receipt append, or promotion.
Checkpoint writes are atomic and invalid state transitions fail explicitly.

proposals.title: Route safely between local intelligence providers
proposals.rationale: The brainstem and authenticated Copilot CLI should share health checks and controlled failover while the deterministic referee remains authoritative.
acceptance: A preflight reports availability and configuration status for brainstem and Copilot CLI providers.
Failover occurs only for classified availability or transient failures and follows configured provider order.
Validation failures never trigger provider failover or bypass the referee.
Receipts identify every attempted provider, failure category, and provider that produced the accepted proposal.

proposals.title: Expose orchestration status and decision traces
proposals.rationale: Operators need a concise local view of current stage, budgets, provider routing, council gate decisions, and failures to supervise autonomy effectively.
acceptance: A non-mutating status command reports run ID, snapshot, stage, provider, remaining budget, and latest gate decision.
A trace command links council input, execution manifest, provider attempts, referee result, and resulting receipt by digest.
Status output is available as stable JSON.
Sensitive credentials and raw authentication output never appear in status or traces.

proposals.title: Cryptographically signed evolution provenance
proposals.rationale: Digest addressing and hash chaining protect integrity but do not establish who authorized a council decision, evolution, artifact build, or promotion. Signed receipts with an explicit trust policy make provenance independently verifiable and fail closed.
acceptance: Every promotable receipt includes a canonical payload digest, signer identity, signature, and signing scheme.
tools/verify_ledger.py exits nonzero for missing, invalid, or untrusted signatures.
Changing any signed receipt or referenced artifact causes verification to fail.
Promotion accepts only signatures from identities permitted by a repository trust policy.

proposals.title: Least-privilege GitHub workflows
proposals.rationale: Issue ingestion, scheduled mutation, and promotion cross from untrusted repository inputs into write-capable GitHub automation. Explicitly separating trust levels limits token exposure and supply-chain compromise.
acceptance: Every workflow declares explicit minimal permissions, with write permissions granted only to the job that requires them.
Every third-party GitHub Action is pinned to a full commit SHA.
Untrusted issue or pull-request content is never evaluated as shell code and cannot reach write credentials.
Promotion requires a protected environment while validation jobs run without repository write access.

proposals.title: Capability-gated reach-up operations
proposals.rationale: The brainstem, Copilot provider, public submission, and promotion paths span distinct trust boundaries. A default-deny capability policy prevents proposal-generation code from inheriting publishing powers, secrets, or unrestricted filesystem access.
acceptance: Proposal providers receive only an allowlisted environment and repository-confined inputs.
Submission and promotion require separate explicit capabilities and fail before side effects when absent.
Provider subprocesses cannot invoke publishing operations or access unrelated credential variables.
Every denied or exercised reach-up capability produces an auditable structured event.

proposals.title: Atomic revision-checked ledger updates
proposals.rationale: Concurrent scheduled or local writers can otherwise produce divergent frontiers or partially updated derived artifacts despite valid individual hashes. Compare-and-swap updates preserve one authoritative lineage and reject stale mutations.
acceptance: Every mutation supplies the expected frontier revision and fails without modifying files when it is stale.
Two concurrent attempts against one revision cannot both append accepted successors.
A simulated interruption during persistence leaves all canonical ledger and frontier files at either the old or new complete state.
Workflow concurrency configuration prevents overlapping repository mutation jobs.

proposals.title: Contain untrusted Moment and card content
proposals.rationale: Gists, issues, peers, cards, and Moment metadata ultimately reach generated state and browser pages. Provenance pinning, resource limits, safe DOM rendering, and iframe isolation prevent mutable-source substitution and active-content injection.
acceptance: Ingested external content records an immutable source revision and verified content digest.
Ingestion rejects unsupported origins, redirects outside the allowlist, oversized payloads, and malformed documents without mutating repository state.
Untrusted strings are rendered as text rather than HTML, and unsafe URL schemes are rejected.
Hologram iframes use restrictive sandbox, referrer, and origin policies enforced by a Content Security Policy.

proposals.title: Player-Directed Evolution
proposals.rationale: Turn the existing three-draft Evolution Lab into a meaningful choice loop by letting players choose an objective, inspect tradeoffs, and select the candidate submitted to the referee.
acceptance: Players can choose motion, articulation, radiance, or balanced objectives before generating drafts.
Identical frontier revision, objective, and seed produce identical candidate digests and ordering.
The UI displays component-level fitness deltas for every draft and requires explicit selection.
Only referee-approved selections persist, and receipts record the objective, seed, all draft digests, and selected digest.

proposals.title: Three-Creature Team Battles
proposals.rationale: Use existing genome and fitness dimensions as complementary combat roles so collection choices and matchup strategy matter beyond a single scalar strength ranking.
acceptance: Players can build a team of exactly three distinct active creatures.
Battle attributes are derived deterministically from canonical genome and fitness data.
Identical teams, ruleset version, and seed produce the same winner and event log in Python and the browser.
Battle replays expose every scoring event and fail verification after any payload modification.

proposals.title: Seeded Evolution Challenge Runs
proposals.rationale: Create replayable finite runs where every player receives the same starting frontier, mutation budget, and objectives, making evolutionary decisions directly comparable.
acceptance: A challenge pins its starting frontier revision, fitness version, seed, objective sequence, and jump limit.
Challenge runs are isolated from the canonical active frontier until explicitly submitted.
The score formula is versioned and combines floor improvement, vitality, and lineage diversity.
A shared run code reproduces the same starting state, objectives, final score, and receipt digest.

proposals.title: Ranked Seasons and Leaderboards
proposals.rationale: Give verified challenge results durable competitive stakes through time-boxed ladders, deterministic standings, and immutable champion archives.
acceptance: Only verified receipts matching the season ruleset and pinned challenge revision enter standings.
Standings sort by score, then fewer jumps, then earliest verified submission.
Closing a season creates an immutable champion snapshot while preserving prior seasons.
The Observatory displays current rank, personal best, season countdown, and archived champions.

proposals.title: Achievement and Collection Progression
proposals.rationale: Reward experimentation, lineage discovery, challenge mastery, and competitive milestones with persistent goals that make the Holodex worth revisiting.
acceptance: Achievement definitions have stable IDs, explicit thresholds, and versioned criteria.
Progress is recomputed from verified evolution, challenge, and battle receipts rather than mutable counters.
The Holodex shows locked requirements, current progress, unlock state, and completion percentage.
Exporting and importing a player profile preserves unlocked achievements and rejects altered receipts.

proposals.title: Accessible interaction baseline
proposals.rationale: The iframe-heavy, animated experiences need consistent keyboard, screen-reader, focus, contrast, and reduced-motion behavior.
acceptance: Automated accessibility scans report zero critical or serious violations on every public page.
Every interactive control is keyboard reachable, visibly focused, and operable without a pointer.
Hologram iframes have descriptive titles and status changes use appropriate live regions.
prefers-reduced-motion disables nonessential animation without hiding content or controls.

proposals.title: Mobile-responsive layouts and controls
proposals.rationale: Dense cards, evolutionary data, and the 3D arena must remain usable on narrow screens and touch-only devices.
acceptance: Pages have no unintended horizontal overflow at 320px, 375px, 768px, and 1024px widths.
Primary controls have touch targets of at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels.
Arena interactions support touch input without requiring hover or a hardware keyboard.
Content and controls remain usable in both portrait and landscape orientations.

proposals.title: Viewport-aware hologram rendering
proposals.rationale: Loading many live hologram iframes and continuous animations can exhaust mobile CPU, memory, and bandwidth before users reach the content.
acceptance: Offscreen hologram players are not instantiated until they approach the viewport.
Players stop animation when offscreen or when the document is hidden and resume when visible.
The arena caps device-pixel-ratio rendering and avoids duplicate animation loops.
Representative mobile runs achieve LCP below 2.5 seconds and CLS below 0.1 using deterministic test fixtures.

proposals.title: Resilient loading, empty, and error states
proposals.rationale: Data-backed pages need clear progress and recovery paths when cards, frontier, lineage, or receipt assets are unavailable or malformed.
acceptance: Each data-backed view displays distinct loading, empty, success, and failure states.
Failures expose an actionable retry control and a concise accessible error message.
Malformed or unavailable JSON produces no uncaught promise rejection and does not leave enabled controls that cannot work.
Loading and failure state changes are announced to assistive technology without moving keyboard focus unexpectedly.

proposals.title: Privacy-safe frontend observability
proposals.rationale: Structured client diagnostics are needed to identify failed data loads, hologram rendering failures, and slow experiences across static pages without exposing Moment payloads.
acceptance: Pages emit structured diagnostics for readiness, data-load success or failure, hologram failure, and render duration.
Diagnostics include page, event, duration, and build revision but exclude Moment tokens, genome payloads, and user-entered content.
No telemetry network request occurs unless an explicit sink is configured.
Injected test sinks can assert emitted events for successful loads, failed fetches, and failed iframe loads.

proposals.title: Robust Fitness Gate
proposals.rationale: A public deterministic scalar can be optimized directly. Accepting on a conservative score across reproducible perturbations rewards durable behavior instead of brittle metric exploits.
acceptance: Parent and child are evaluated with the same versioned deterministic probe set covering phase shifts, temporal resampling, and bounded genome perturbations.
A jump passes only when the child's lower-quartile probe score exceeds the parent's by the configured margin.
Each receipt records the probe version, seed, individual scores, aggregate, and enough data for byte-identical replay.

proposals.title: Quality-Diversity Archive
proposals.rationale: Repeatedly maximizing one scalar invites a monoculture of similarly frenetic organisms. Behavior niches preserve genuinely different high-quality motion, articulation, and radiance strategies without weakening fitness standards.
acceptance: Every valid Moment receives deterministic behavior descriptors and a stable niche identifier derived from its 100-frame phenotype.
A candidate fills an empty niche above the quality floor or replaces its niche elite only by exceeding the configured margin.
Synthetic populations retain equally fit but behaviorally distinct candidates while rejecting descriptor-near duplicates.
Receipts and the observatory expose niche, novelty distance, and admission or replacement reason.

proposals.title: Versioned Fitness Contract Benchmark
proposals.rationale: Fitness changes need semantic regression tests, not only implementation parity. An immutable benchmark makes intended rankings and invariances explicit and prevents a new epoch from silently rewarding known pathologies.
acceptance: The benchmark defines versioned expected rankings and invariances for translation, cyclic time origin, redundant encoding, precision jitter, isolated spikes, and saturated noise.
The audit command reports every violated contract and exits nonzero when any mandatory contract fails.
CI requires a fitness-version bump when algorithm or profile constants change and evaluates the proposed epoch against all contracts.
Benchmark results include score deltas and fixture digests so failures are reproducible.

proposals.title: Counterfactual Fitness Scorecards
proposals.rationale: A total score alone cannot explain why an organism won or reveal which term is being exploited. Decomposed and counterfactual scorecards make decisions inspectable by developers and viewers.
acceptance: Every evaluation emits raw features, normalized components, gates, couplings, penalties, profile digest, and final score.
The serialized components reproduce the final score within 1e-9 and remain parity-consistent with the browser implementation.
Accepted and rejected comparisons include child-minus-parent deltas plus deterministic channel-ablation counterfactuals.
The observatory renders a score waterfall and identifies the largest positive and negative contributors without recomputing receipt data.

proposals.title: Population Fitness Health Monitor
proposals.rationale: The floor can rise while diversity, lineage balance, or component quality collapses. Population-level health metrics expose Goodhart drift that individual acceptance checks cannot detect.
acceptance: Each frontier revision records descriptor coverage, nearest-neighbor novelty, lineage concentration, component saturation, and score-component correlations.
Configurable thresholds flag niche collapse, dominant lineages, and sustained saturation before scheduled promotion proceeds.
Known healthy and collapsed fixture populations respectively pass and fail the audit deterministically.
The observatory shows health trends by revision and links each alert to contributing niches, lineages, or fitness components.

proposals.title: Atomic Evolution Transactions
proposals.rationale: A jump updates several authoritative and derived JSON files. A crash or concurrent writer can otherwise expose a partially committed frontier, receipt chain, or artifact set.
acceptance: Each accepted jump commits all authoritative state under one stable operation ID.
Fault injection after every write phase recovers to either the exact pre-transaction or post-transaction state.
Concurrent writers cannot interleave updates; conflicts retry against the latest frontier or fail explicitly.
Replaying a committed operation ID is a byte-for-byte no-op.

proposals.title: Repository Integrity Gate
proposals.rationale: Hash-chain verification alone does not guarantee consistency among the frontier, observations, lineages, cards, indexes, and rebuilt artifacts. CI should enforce all cross-file invariants before publication.
acceptance: One read-only command validates schemas, digests, receipt links, lineage references, active and retired partitions, champion, and floor.
CI fails when deterministic artifact regeneration produces a tracked diff.
Corrupting each protected invariant in fixtures produces a nonzero exit with the failing path and invariant.
A valid repository snapshot passes without modifying files.

proposals.title: Serialized Scheduled Writes
proposals.rationale: Scheduled harness and ingestion jobs can mutate overlapping generated state. Shared serialization and optimistic revision checks prevent lost updates and stale-frontier commits.
acceptance: Every workflow that commits generated state uses one shared concurrency group with active jobs never canceled.
Writers verify their base revision immediately before publication and recompute after a stale-revision conflict.
A bounded two-writer integration test preserves both valid operations without overwriting either result.
Repeated delivery of the same scheduled or issue event produces no duplicate observation or receipt.

proposals.title: Bounded External Retry Policy
proposals.rationale: Brainstem, Copilot CLI, GitHub, and promotion operations cross unreliable process or network boundaries. Centralized deadlines and classified retries prevent hangs, retry storms, and duplicate mutations.
acceptance: Transient timeouts, rate limits, and server errors retry with bounded exponential backoff and honor Retry-After.
Validation, authentication, and other permanent failures are not retried.
Every external operation has a total deadline, attempt limit, and stable idempotency key.
Deterministic tests cover eventual success, deadline exhaustion, permanent failure, and duplicate-response handling.

proposals.title: Structured Operational Telemetry
proposals.rationale: Scheduled autonomous changes need durable diagnostics beyond console text so operators can distinguish accepted jumps, deduplicated no-ops, provider failures, integrity failures, and publication conflicts.
acceptance: Every run emits a schema-versioned JSON report on success, no-op, rejection, and failure.
Reports include run ID, repository revision, challenge ID, fitness version, outcome, durations, before and after digests, and receipt ID when available.
Workflows publish the report and a concise job summary even when a step fails.
Tests prove reports exclude credentials, raw authentication output, and unredacted provider payloads.

proposals.title: Council Proposal Lifecycle
proposals.rationale: Turn recurring council output into a durable roadmap with explicit decisions, bounded work in progress, and shipped-feature exclusion so autonomous strategy produces progress rather than duplicate ideas.
acceptance: Repeated council runs merge proposals sharing a feature_id instead of creating duplicate roadmap entries.
Each proposal supports proposed, planned, shipped, and declined states with an immutable decision record.
Shipped feature IDs are included in subsequent council snapshots as completed features.
The observatory displays proposal priority, scope, status, source revision, and decision rationale.

proposals.title: Public Challenge Submission Flow
proposals.rationale: Connect the existing Evolution Lab, revision-bound challenges, and issue ingestion into one contributor journey, making Double Jump participatory without introducing accounts or a hosted backend.
acceptance: The lab exports a canonical submission bundle containing the frontier revision, challenge ID, candidate genome, and digest.
A contributor can open the submission issue and provide the generated bundle without manually authoring JSON.
Ingestion reports distinct actionable errors for stale challenges, invalid schemas, duplicate content, and insufficient jump margins.
Accepted submissions preserve the immutable parent and record contributor and challenge provenance in the evolution receipt.

proposals.title: Unified Product Navigation
proposals.rationale: The gallery, arena, lab, observatory, and Holodex describe one improvement loop but appear as separate surfaces. A shared journey and vocabulary would make the product understandable without reducing its distinctive depth.
acceptance: Every public page renders the same primary navigation with a correct active state.
The landing page offers clear Explore, Evolve, and Integrate entry paths.
A visible Observe, Challenge, Evolve, Verify journey links each stage to its corresponding surface.
Creature and lineage selections remain addressable when navigating between supporting pages.

proposals.title: Canonical Creature Detail Page
proposals.rationale: Unify card art, fitness, frontier state, ancestry, and receipts around one content-addressed creature identity so users can understand what evolved and share a durable product-level URL.
acceptance: A digest-addressed URL renders the creature hologram, metadata, active or retired state, fitness version, and score breakdown.
The page displays parent and child lineage links plus associated evolution receipts.
Arena, Holodex, and observatory entries link to the same canonical creature URL.
Unknown or malformed digests produce an explicit not-found state without substituting another creature.

proposals.title: Versioned Federation Manifest
proposals.rationale: Formalize peer discovery and compatibility around a small static manifest, strengthening federation while preserving the repository's serverless and sustainable operating model.
acceptance: Artifact generation emits a versioned manifest containing canonical origin, supported schemas, feed URLs, revision, and checksums.
The Holodex resolves configured peers through their manifests and validates compatible schemas before displaying remote cards.
Identical content digests from multiple peers render once while retaining every origin.
Unavailable, malformed, or incompatible peers are isolated and shown with a diagnostic status without blocking local content.
